name: Yirgacheffe Kochere
tastingNote: Delicate and tea-like with a floral jasmine nose, vibrant bergamot acidity, and a silky lemon curd finish. One of the most aromatic coffees on the planet.

name: Guji Natural
tastingNote: A wild, wine-like natural that bursts with blueberry compote and dark cherry on the palate, underscored by bittersweet dark chocolate and a lingering lavender note.

name: Huila Honey
tastingNote: Round and sweet with juicy peach and apricot, melting into brown sugar and milk chocolate through the mid-palate. The honey process adds a velvety body that rounds everything out beautifully.

name: Nariño Washed
tastingNote: Crisp and bright with red apple and lime zest acidity, transitioning to creamy caramel sweetness and a gentle hazelnut finish. A textbook example of high-altitude Colombian excellence.

name: Nyeri AA
tastingNote: Boldly structured with a distinctive blackcurrant and tomato-like savouriness, followed by grapefruit tartness and a clean cranberry finish. Intensely complex and unmistakably Kenyan.

name: Kirinyaga Washed
tastingNote: More delicate than classic Nyeri lots, with raspberry jam sweetness, lemon verbena brightness, and a long floral aftertaste reminiscent of a fine Darjeeling.

name: Huehuetenango SHB
tastingNote: A well-balanced, crowd-pleasing cup with toffee sweetness, crisp apple acidity, and a roasted almond nuttiness. The dried apricot note lingers warmly through the finish.

name: Tarrazu Anaerobic
tastingNote: An adventurous anaerobic that leads with explosive passion fruit and tropical aromatics. Hibiscus tea acidity and a grape-skin finish make this one for the experimentally minded.

name: Sumatra Mandheling
tastingNote: Full-bodied and syrupy, with distinctive earthy cedar and tobacco notes underscored by bittersweet dark chocolate. The wet-hulled process gives this an unmistakably Indonesian character.

name: Geisha Natural
tastingNote: The legendary Geisha varietal at its most expressive. Peach nectar and orange blossom perfume the cup, with white tea delicacy and a long honey sweetness that simply refuses to fade.

name: Kayanza Washed
tastingNote: Bright and clean with red plum sweetness, blood orange acidity, and a comforting brown sugar finish. Exceptional clarity and sweetness make this a great introduction to East African coffees.

name: Nyungwe Honey
tastingNote: Soft and fruit-forward, with caramel apple and nectarine sweetness balanced by a subtle vanilla creaminess. The honey process adds texture without losing the natural clarity of the varietal.

name: Cajamarca Natural
tastingNote: A rich, fruit-forward natural from Peru's northern highlands. Fresh strawberry and molasses sweetness lead, with a walnut and milk chocolate backbone that makes it an ideal everyday drinker.

name: Marcala Carbonic
tastingNote: A wildly experimental carbonic maceration with strawberry soda aromatics and rose petal sweetness. Guava and lime acidity make it effervescent and unlike anything else in the bag. Best black.

name: Chiapas Oaxaca Blend
tastingNote: A comforting blend of two great Mexican regions. Dark chocolate and toasted almond dominate, with a bright orange peel top note and a whisper of cinnamon that makes it perfect for the morning.

name: Mokha Port Natural
tastingNote: An ancient coffee with a story to match. Dried fig sweetness, tamarind tartness, and cacao nib depth are woven through a woody, almost smoky base that speaks to centuries of unbroken cultivation.
